en.extract:
The Bornean barbet (Psilopogon eximius) is a species of bird in the Megalaimidae family. It is found in Indonesia and Malaysia where it is endemic to the island of Borneo. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''Bornean barbet''' ('''''Psilopogon eximius''''') is a [[species]] of [[bird]] in the [[Megalaimidae]] family. It is found in [[Indonesia]] and [[Malaysia]] where it is [[endemism|endemic]] to the island of [[Borneo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Q. |author2=Phillipps, K. |name-list-style=amp |year=2011 |series= |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 |pages= |url= }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical]] or tropical [[Borneo lowland rain forests|moist lowland forests]] and subtropical or tropical [[Borneo montane rain forests|moist montane forests]].
